The Classification, Labelling and Packaging of Chemicals (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015
The Optical Charges and Payments (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Health and Personal Social Services (Optical Charges and Payments) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997 (“the principal Regulations”) which provide for payment to be made, by means of a voucher system, in respect of costs incurred by certain categories of persons in connection with the supply, replacement and repair of optical appliances.

items.title: The Waiting Restrictions (Clogher) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
The Cycle Routes (Amendment No. 2) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
The Traffic Weight Restriction (Amendment) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
Provision of Health Services to Persons Not Ordinarily Resident Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: These Regulations revoke and replace the Provision of Health Services to Persons not Ordinarily Resident Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2005. The Regulations make available, to visitors in Northern Ireland, certain health services under the Health and Personal Social Services (Northern Ireland) Order 1972, at a charge determined by the Department of Health, Social Services and Public Safety,  “the Department”. The Regulations go on to provide for various exemptions from charges: some services are always exempt from charge, some categories of visitor are exempt from charge.

items.title: The Parking Places on Roads and Waiting Restrictions (Newry) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
The Local Government Reorganisation (Compensation for Loss of Employment) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: These Regulations amend the Local Government Reorganisation (Compensation for Loss of Employment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015 to include the Local Government Staff Commission as a local government body and to clarify, in regulation 5, that a calculation of compensation payment may take into account a payment under the Civil Service Pension Scheme.

items.title: The Funded Public Service Pension Schemes (Reduction of Cash Equivalents) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: A funded public service defined benefits scheme can be designated under section 93A of the Pension Schemes (Northern Ireland) Act 1993 c. 49 (‘the 1993 Act’) if a relevant Department considers that (a) there is an increased likelihood of payments out of public funds, or of increased payments out of public funds, having to be made into the scheme so that it can meet its liabilities, and (b) the increased likelihood is connected with the exercise or expected future exercise of rights to take a cash equivalent. When a member requires the trustees or managers of a scheme to use a cash equivalent for acquiring a right or entitlement to flexible benefits under the rules of another pension scheme, and the scheme was already designated on the date that the member makes his or her application under section 91(1) of the 1993 Act, these Regulations require the trustees or managers to reduce the cash equivalent. They also set out how the amount of the reduction is to be determined. The reduction must not however be made if the scheme ceases to be designated before the date on which the trustees or managers do what is needed to carry out what the member requires.

items.title: The Planning (Development Management) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: Regulation 2 amends the Schedule to the Planning (Development Management) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015 to correct a drafting error in those Regulations.

items.title: The Smoke Control Areas (Exempted Fireplaces) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: The Smoke Control Areas (Exempted Fireplaces) (No. 2) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2013 (“the 2013 Regulations”) specify those fireplaces exempted from the provisions of Article 17(7) of the Clean Air (Northern Ireland) Order 1981.

items.title: The Parking and Waiting Restrictions (Belfast) (Amendment No. 2) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
The Industrial Training Levy (Construction Industry) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: This Order gives effect to proposals submitted by the Construction Industry Training Board to the Department for Employment and Learning for the imposition of a further levy upon employers in the construction industry for the purpose of raising money towards the expenses of the Board.

items.title: The Transport (2011 Act) (Commencement No.2) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: This is the second Commencement Order to be made under the Transport Act (Northern Ireland) 2011.

items.title: The Controlled Drugs (Supervision of Management and Use) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: These Controlled Drugs (Supervision of Management and Use) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015, “the Regulations”, amend the Controlled Drugs (Supervision of Management and Use) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2009, “the principal Regulations”.

items.title: The Marine Conservation (Fixed Monetary Penalties) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: This Order provides the Department of the Environment with the powers to apply fixed monetary penalties on persons who have contravened byelaws made under section 26 of the Marine Act (Northern Ireland) 2013 c. 10 (the Act).

items.title: The Local Government (2014 Act) (Commencement No. 3) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: This Order brings into operation section 5(1) (disqualification of holders of prescribed offices and appointments) of the Local Government Act (Northern Ireland) 2014. Section 5(2) was commenced by the Local Government (2014 Act) (Commencement No. 2) Order 2014 (2014 No. 153 (C. 8).

items.title: The Planning (Amount of Fixed Penalty) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: These Regulations prescribe the amount of the penalty payable under a fixed penalty notice served under section 153(9) or 154(9) of the Planning Act (Northern Ireland) 2011.

items.title: The Social Security (Application of Reciprocal Agreements with Australia, Canada and New Zealand) ( EEA States and Switzerland) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015 (Revoked with saving)
items.description: These Regulations modify the operation of the Social Security (Australia) Order (Northern Ireland) 1992 (“the Australia Order”), the Social Security (Canada) Order (Northern Ireland) 1995, and the Social Security (New Zealand) Order (Northern Ireland) 1983 which give effect in Northern Ireland to reciprocal agreements on social security made between the Governments of those respective countries and the Government of the United Kingdom.

items.title: The Non-Commercial Movement of Pet Animals (Amendment) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: This Order amends the Non-Commercial Movement of Pet Animals Order (Northern Ireland) 2011(“the 2011 Order”) to update the enforcement provisions and import requirements for pet animals entering Northern Ireland as a result of Regulation (EU) No 576/2013 of the European Parliament and of the Council on the non-commercial movement of pet animals and repealing Regulation (EC) no 998/2003 (OJ No L 178, 28.06.2013, p.1-26) (“the Pets Regulation”).

items.title: The Road Races (Armoy Motorcycle Race) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
The Transport (2011 Act) (Commencement No.3) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: This is the third Commencement Order to be made under the Transport Act (Northern Ireland) 2011.

items.title: The Public Passenger Transport (Service Agreements and Service Permits) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: These Regulations lay down the rules for Part 1 of the Transport Act (Northern Ireland) 2011 (“the Act”) in relation to the provision of public passenger transport services and do so in accordance with the requirements of Commission Regulation (EC) No. 1370/2007 on public passenger transport services by rail and road.

items.title: The Common Agricultural Policy Direct Payments and Support Schemes (Cross Compliance) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: These Regulations amend the Common Agricultural Policy Single Payment and Support Schemes (Cross Compliance) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2014 (S.R. 2014 No. 291) (“the principal Regulations”). The principal Regulations supplemented and made provision for the administration and enforcement of Regulation (EU) No. 1306/2013 (O.J. No L347, 20.12.13, p. 549), Commission Delegated Regulation (EU) No 640/2014 and Commission Implementing Regulation (EU) No 809/2014 in relation to cross compliance under the revised system of direct support schemes under the common agricultural policy which came into force on 1st January 2015.

items.title: The Renewables Obligation (Amendment) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: This Order amends the Renewables Obligation Order (Northern Ireland) 2009 (“the 2009 Order”) and makes transitional provision.

items.title: The Hazardous Waste (Amendment No. 2) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: These Regulations are made under Section 2(2) of and paragraph 1A of Schedule 2 to the European Communities Act 1972. They amend a number of pieces of Northern Ireland legislation to bring into effect the changes introduced by Commission Decision 2014/955/EU amending Decision 2000/532/EC (the Decision) on the list of waste pursuant to Directive 2008/98/EC of the European Parliament and of the Council. Commission Decision 2000/532/EC is directly binding as regards determination of waste that is to be considered as hazardous waste. The changes being introduced by way of the updated Decision came into effect on 1 June 2015.

items.title: The Local Government (Exclusion of Non-commercial Considerations) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: Article 19(1) of the Local Government (Miscellaneous Provisions) (Northern Ireland) Order 1992 (the 1992 Order) provides that (subject to Article 20 (fair employment matters), 20A (race relations matters) and 21(1) provisions supplementary to or consequential on Article 19), in exercising any of the functions mentioned in paragraph (3) of that Article in relation to public supply or works contracts, any proposed or subsisting such contract, district councils must exercise these functions without reference to certain non-commercial matters listed in paragraph (4) of that Article.

items.title: The Police (Northern Ireland) Act 2000 (Designated Places of Detention: Lay Visitors) Order 2015
items.description: Under section 73 of the Police (Northern Ireland) Act 2000 lay visitors may only visit a place of detention designated by the Secretary of State under Paragraph 1 of Schedule 8 to the Terrorism Act 2000 (to hold terrorist suspects or persons detained under Schedule 7 to that Act), if it has been designated by Order as a place they may visit.

items.title: The Criminal Justice (Northern Ireland) Order 2008 (Commencement No. 9) Order 2015
items.description: This Order brings into operation Articles 22, 24(2), 24(7)(a), 26(2), 28 and 29 of the Criminal Justice (Northern Ireland) Order 2008 on 1 July 2015. These provisions provide for the release and recall of certain categories of prisoners, and for licence conditions to be placed upon them including a curfew. They also provide for the release of certain categories of prisoners on compassionate grounds and for the further release of persons released under Article 20 and subsequently recalled.

items.title: The Road Races (POC NI Oils Stages Rally) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
The Road Races (Ulster Grand Prix Bike Week) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
The Roads (Speed Limit) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
The Teachers’ Superannuation (Additional Voluntary Contributions) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: These regulations amend the Teachers’ Superannuation (Additional Voluntary Contributions) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996 (“the 1996 Regulations”) to permit members of the Teachers’ Pension Scheme 2015 access to the additional voluntary contributions scheme (“the scheme”), to enable members to utilise the additional flexibilities for defined contribution pension schemes provided for in the Pension Schemes Act 2015 and to take account of changes to tax treatment of individuals’ pension funds introduced by the Taxation of Pensions Act 2014.

items.title: The Roads (Speed Limit) (No. 2) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
The Criminal Justice (2013 Act) (Commencement No.5) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: This Order brings into operation section 13(1) of the Criminal Justice Act (Northern Ireland) 2013 (c.7) (“the 2013 Act”).

items.title: The Bus Lanes (Upper Newtownards Road, Belfast – between Sandown Road and Knock Road) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
The Prohibition of U-Turn (A2 Belfast Road, Carrickfergus) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
The Parking Places on Roads (Medical Practitioners) (Amendment) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
The Public Service Pensions (2014 Act) (Commencement No.2) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: This Order brings into operation certain provisions of the Public Service Pensions Act (Northern Ireland) 2014 (c.2). This is the second commencement order to be made under that Act.

items.title: The Rathdown Walk, Lisburn (Abandonment) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
The Parking and Waiting Restrictions (Belfast) (Amendment No. 3) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
The Waste Management Licensing (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: These Regulations amend regulations 3 to 5 of and Schedule 1 to the Waste Management Licensing Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2003 (the 2003 Regulations) and prescribe the accepted technical competencies for the purpose of determining if a person can be deemed a fit and proper person to hold a waste management licence.

items.title: The Lands Tribunal (Amendment) Rules (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: These Rules amend the Lands Tribunal Rules (Northern Ireland) 1976 (S.R. 1976 No. 146) to amend the fees for appeals in relation to an entry in a NAV list and for copies of documents.

items.title: The One-Way Traffic (Ballymena) (Amendment) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
The Waiting Restrictions (Cookstown) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
The Control of Traffic (Lisburn) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
The A29 Moy Road and U7004 Drumcairn Road, Armagh (Abandonment) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
The Pensions (2015 Act) (Commencement No. 1) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: This Order provides for the coming into operation of certain provisions of the Pensions Act (Northern Ireland) 2015 (“the Act”) in accordance with Article 2.

items.title: The Pensions (2015 Act) (Consequential Amendments) (Units of Additional Pension) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: This is the first rule to be made under section 50 of the Pensions Act (Northern Ireland) 2015. This Order makes consequential amendments to primary and secondary legislation as a result of the introduction of increased additional pension attributable to units of additional pension. A person may pay a Class 3A voluntary National Insurance contribution in return for a unit of additional pension which results in an increase in the weekly rate of the additional pension in a Category A, B or D retirement pension.

items.title: The Occupational Pension Schemes (Charges and Governance) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: These Regulations impose obligations on trustees and managers of certain occupational pension schemes in relation to charges imposed by and governance of those schemes. They are the first regulations to be made under Schedule 18 to the Pensions Act (Northern Ireland) 2015 (“the 2015 Act”).

items.title: The Urban Clearways (Amendment) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
The Occupational and Personal Pension Schemes (Automatic Enrolment) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: These Regulations amend the Occupational and Personal Pension Schemes (Automatic Enrolment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2010 (“the 2010 Regulations”) by reducing the information requirements on employers, introducing certain circumstances where the employer duty is turned into a discretion and prescribing alternative quality requirements for defined benefits schemes.

items.title: The Social Security (Units of Additional Pension) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: These Regulations specify the amount a person will receive in return for each unit of additional pension as a result of paying a Class 3A voluntary National Insurance contribution. The payment of Class 3A contributions is provided for by sections 14A to 14C of the Social Security Contributions and Benefits (Northern Ireland) Act 1992 (“the Contributions and Benefits Act”), as inserted by paragraph 17 of Schedule 15 to the Pensions Act 2014 (c. 19). Section 25 of, and Schedule 15 to, the Pensions Act (Northern Ireland) 2015 provide for the resulting payment of extra additional State Pension.

items.title: The Road Races (Ulster Rally) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
The Road Races (Garron Point Hill Climb) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
The Parking Places on Roads (Disabled Persons’ Vehicles) (Amendment No. 2) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
The State Pension Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: The Pensions Act (Northern Ireland) 2015 (“the Act”), creates a new state pension for people reaching pensionable age on or after 6th April 2016 (see Part 1 of the Act). It replaces the existing state pension. These Regulations set out some of the detailed rules relating to the new state pension.

items.title: The Parking Places and Waiting Restrictions (Ballymoney) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
The Parking Places on Roads (Lisburn City Centre) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
The Common Agricultural Policy (Review of Decisions) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations enable the Department to establish procedures for the review of a relevant determination made by the Department under any of the Community instruments listed in the Schedule to the Regulations, or any statutory provision which implements or supplements those Community instruments in Northern Ireland.

items.title: The Pensions (2005 Order) (Code of Practice) (Funding Defined Benefits) (Appointed Day) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: This Order appoints the day for the purposes of Article 86(7) of the Pensions (Northern Ireland) Order 2005 for the coming into operation of the Pensions Regulator Code of practice no. 3: Funding defined benefits: Second issue as 29th July 2015.

items.title: The Valuation for Rating (Decapitalisation Rate) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: These Regulations prescribe the decapitalisation rate to be applied when determining the net annual value of any hereditament by reference to the contractor’s principle for the purposes of any NAV list coming into force on or after 1st April 2015. The contractor’s principle is the method of ascertaining the net annual value of a hereditament by reference to its actual or notional cost of construction or provision or to its capital value.

items.title: The Justice (2015 Act) (Commencement No. 1) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: This Order brings sections 28, 29 and 31 – 35 of the Justice Act (Northern Ireland) 2015 (c.9) into operation on 1 September 2015.

items.title: The Country of Origin of Certain Meats Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: These Regulations make provision to enforce certain provisions of Commission Implementing Regulation (EU) No 1337/2013 laying down rules for the application of Regulation (EU) No 1169/2011 of the European Parliament and of the Council as regards the indication of the country of origin or place of provenance for fresh, chilled and frozen meat of swine, sheep, goats and poultry (OJ No L 335, 14.12.13, p 19) (“the Commission Regulation”).

items.title: The Tuberculosis (Examination and Testing) Scheme (Amendment) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: This Order amends the Schedule to the Tuberculosis (Examination and Testing) Order (Northern Ireland) 1999. It provides in paragraph 1 for a new definition of an approved veterinary surgeon, who may carry out official tests for tuberculosis on animals, and amends the definition of “authorised officer” and “official test”.

items.title: The Road Races (Bushwhacker Rally) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
The Justice (2015 Act) (Commencement No. 2) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: This Order brings into operation certain provisions of the Justice Act (Northern Ireland) 2015 (c.9 (N.I)) (“the Act”).

items.title: The Control of Major Accident Hazards Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015
The Rural Development Programme Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: These Regulations make provision for the implementation in Northern Ireland of the European Regulations (as defined in Schedule 1) relating to the administration of the Rural Development Programme. The Rural Development Programme 2014 - 2020 was approved by European Commission Decision C(2015) 6018 of 25th August 2015 and can be downloaded from http://www.dardni.gov.uk/2014-2020-rdp-version-1.2.pdf. The provisions in the EU legislation are directly applicable and have direct effect in a member state. These Regulations provide a domestic legal framework for the operation of the EU Regulations in Northern Ireland.

items.title: The Local Government (Performance Indicators and Standards) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: This Order specifies performance indicators and standards by reference to which a council’s performance in exercising a related function can be measured or met.

items.title: The Unfunded Public Service Defined Benefits Schemes (Transfers) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: These Regulations prevent transfers under section 91 of the Pension Schemes (Northern Ireland) Act 1993 (‘the 1993 Act’) from an unfunded public service defined benefits scheme to a qualifying recognised overseas pension scheme which can provide flexible benefits as a result of the transfer.

items.title: The Pensions (2015 Act) (Commencement No. 2) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: This Order provides for the coming into operation of certain provisions of the Pensions Act (Northern Ireland) 2015 (“the Act”) in accordance with Article 2.

items.title: The Parking Places on Roads (Disabled Persons’ Vehicles) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
The Disclosure of Victims' and Witnesses' Information (Prescribed Bodies) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: Paragraph 1(1) of Schedule 3 to the Justice Act (Northern Ireland) 2015 makes provision for a police officer or member of the police support staff to disclose relevant information, as set out in paragraph 1(2), relating to a victim to a prescribed body for the purposes of enabling that body to advise the victim about support services provided by the body, or to offer or provide support services to the victim.

items.title: The State Pension Credit (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: These Regulations amend the State Pension Credit Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2003 (“the 2003 Regulations”) so as to limit savings credit for certain mixed-age couples and to bring forward the date on which certain assessed income periods end.

items.title: The Animal By-Products (Enforcement) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: These Regulations revoke and remake The Animal By-Products (Enforcement) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2011 incorporating amendments made by The Animal By-Products (Enforcement) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2011 and The Animal By-Products (Enforcement) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2014 and updating various other provisions.

items.title: The Disability Discrimination Act 1995 (Commencement No. 11) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: This Order provides for the coming into operation of section 32 of the Disability Discrimination Act 1995 which empowers the Department of the Environment to make taxi accessibility Regulations to ensure that disabled persons can get into and out of regulated taxis safely and be carried in safety and reasonable comfort and, in the case of disabled persons in wheelchairs to do so while remaining in their wheelchairs.

items.title: The Roads (Speed Limit) (No. 3) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
The Parking and Waiting Restrictions (Cathedral Quarter, Belfast) (Amendment) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
The Bus Lanes (Falls Road, Belfast – between Grosvenor Road and Whiterock Road) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
The Parking and Waiting Restrictions (Dungannon) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
The Prohibition of Waiting (Amendment No. 2) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
The Genetically Modified Organisms (Contained Use) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015
The Social Security (Miscellaneous Amendments) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: These Regulations amend various sets of social security regulations.

items.title: The Brucellosis Control (Amendment) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: This Order further amends the Brucellosis Control Order (Northern Ireland) 2004 (“the 2004 Order”) by revoking the provisions in Part III of that Order prohibiting the movement of animals into or out of a herd, except directly to a slaughterhouse, unless the animals have been subjected to an official test prior to the date of movement.

items.title: The General Teaching Council for Northern Ireland (Constitution) (Amendment No. 2) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: These Regulations amend the General Teaching Council for Northern Ireland (Constitution) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2001 to extend the term of office of elected Council members whose term would otherwise have ended on 14th October 2015 by a further six months (regulation 2(2)). That regulation also extends the term of office of members appointed by the Department of Education until 29th February 2016. In both cases the term of office of members will end on 14th October 2019. Regulation 2(3) makes minor amendments.

items.title: The Glebe Way, Moira (Abandonment) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
The Parking and Waiting Restrictions (Banbridge) (Amendment) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
The Planning (Hazardous Substances) (No. 2) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: These Regulations implement the land-use planning obligations (articles 13 and 15) in Directive 2012/18/EU of the European Parliament and of the Council on the control of major-accident hazards involving dangerous substances (O.J. 197, 24.7.2012, p.1) (“the Seveso III Directive”).

items.title: The Sexual Offences Act 2003 (Prescribed Police Stations) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015 (revoked)
items.description: The Sexual Offences Act 2003 (“the 2003 Act”) imposes notification requirements on offenders convicted of certain sexual offences. These offenders are called  “relevant offenders”. The 2003 Act requires relevant offenders to notify certain personal information to the police, both at the outset and periodically thereafter. Section 87(1) of the 2003 Act provides that relevant offenders give such notification by attending at any police station in the relevant offender's local police area which is prescribed in regulations. These Regulations prescribe those police stations in Northern Ireland.

items.title: The Renewables Obligation Closure Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: This Order makes provision for no renewables obligation certificates to be issued under a renewables obligation order in respect of electricity generated after 31st March 2017 unless the electricity is generated in any one or more of the circumstances provided for in the Order.

items.title: The Superannuation (Assembly Ombudsman for Northern Ireland and the Northern Ireland Commissioner for Complaints) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015 (revoked)
items.description: This Order adds employment by the Assembly Ombudsman for Northern Ireland and the Northern Ireland Commissioner for Complaints to the kinds of employment listed in Schedule 1 to the Superannuation (Northern Ireland) Order 1972, for which the Department of Finance and Personnel may make pension schemes. It has retrospective effect from 1 July 2015 by virtue of Article 3(7) of the 1972 Order.

items.title: The Public Service Pensions (Assembly Ombudsman for Northern Ireland and the Northern Ireland Commissioner for Complaints) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations specify persons employed by the Assembly Ombudsman for Northern Ireland and the Northern Ireland Commissioner for Complaints as persons to whom the scheme, established under The Public Service (Civil Servants and Others) Pension Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2014, may potentially relate, in accordance with section 25(3) of the Public Service Pensions (Northern Ireland) Act 2014. It has retrospective effect from 1st July 2015 by virtue of section 3(3)(b) of the 2014 Act.

items.title: The Road Traffic (Fixed Penalty) (Offences) (Amendment) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: This Order amends Schedule 1 to the Road Traffic (Fixed Penalty) (Offences) Order (Northern Ireland) 1997 to—

items.title: The Education (2014 Act) (Commencement No.1) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: This Order brings into operation the Sections and Schedules of the Education Act (Northern Ireland) 2014 which dissolve the former Education and Library Boards and commence the associated amendments and repeals.

items.title: The Police Act 1997 (Criminal Records) (Amendments No. 2) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: These Regulations amend the Police Act 1997 (Criminal Records) (Registration) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2007 (“the 2007 Regulations”) and the Police Act 1997 (Criminal Records) (Disclosure) Regulations 2008 (“the 2008 Regulations”).

items.title: The Water Framework Directive (Classification, Priority Substances and Shellfish Waters) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: These Regulations implement Council Directive 2008/105/EC on environmental quality standards in the field of water policy (OJ No. L348, 24.12.2008 p.84) as amended by Directive 2013/39/EU (OJ L226, 24.08.2013, p.1) and set out the classification schemes used under the implementation of Directive 2000/60/EC establishing a framework for Community action in the field of water policy (OJ No. L327, 22.12.2000 p.1-73).

items.title: The Snares Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: This Order makes provision about the use of snares.

items.title: The Criminal Justice (European Protection Order) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations make minor technical amendments to the Criminal Justice (European Protection Order) (Northern Ireland) Regulations 2014.

items.title: The Human Medicines (Amendment) (No.3) Regulations 2015
items.description: These Regulations amend the Human Medicines Regulations 2012 (the 2012 Regulations).

items.title: The New Firefighters’ Pension Scheme (Amendment) (No. 3) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: This Order amends the New Firefighters’ Pension Scheme Order (Northern Ireland) 2007.

items.title: The Attorney General’s Human Rights Guidance (Police Service of Northern Ireland – Protection of Life) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: This Order brings into operation guidance by the Attorney General for Northern Ireland, pursuant to section 8 of the Justice (Northern Ireland) Act 2004, which was laid before the Assembly on 18th September 2015.

items.title: The Motor Vehicles (Driving Licences) (Amendment No. 3) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: These Regulations amend the Motor Vehicle (Driving Licences) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996 (“the 1996 Regulations”) to correct anomalies which exist between the legislation in Great Britain and the legislation in Northern Ireland, with the aim of improving parity of driver licensing law throughout the United Kingdom.

items.title: The Justice (2015 Act) (Commencement No. 3) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: This Order brings into operation certain provisions of the Justice Act (Northern Ireland) 2015 (c.9 (N.I.)) (“the Act”).

items.title: The Legal Aid and Coroners’ Courts (2014 Act) (Commencement No. 2) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: This Order brings into operation certain provisions of the Legal Aid and Coroners’ Courts Act (Northern Ireland) 2014(“the 2014 Act”).

items.title: The Fair Employment (Specification of Public Authorities) (Amendment) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015 (revoked)
items.description: This Order amends the Fair Employment (Specification of Public Authorities) Order (Northern Ireland) 2004 (“the 2004 Order”) which specifies a number of persons or bodies as public authorities for certain purposes under Part VII of the Fair Employment and Treatment (Northern Ireland) Order 1998 (“the 1998 Order”).

items.title: The Police (Appeals, Conduct and Unsatisfactory Performance and Attendance) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: These Regulations amend the definitions of “member” in the Royal Ulster Constabulary (Appeals) Regulations 2000, the Royal Ulster Constabulary (Conduct) Regulations 2000 and the Police (Unsatisfactory Performance and Attendance) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2010.

items.title: The Charities (2008 Act) (Commencement No. 6) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: This Order brings into operation section 65(10)(b) of the Charities Act (Northern Ireland) 2008 which allows the Department to amend the list of bodies whose members may examine the accounts of certain lower-income charities.

items.title: The Attorney General’s Human Rights Guidance (Youth Justice Agency – Restorative Justice) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: This Order brings into operation guidance by the Attorney General for Northern Ireland, pursuant to section 8 of the Justice (Northern Ireland) Act 2004, which was laid before the Assembly on 9th October 2015.

items.title: The Water Supply (Water Quality) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015 (revoked)
items.description: Regulation 2 amends the Water Supply (Water Quality) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2007, as amended, to include an additional analytical parameter (radon), with associated threshold values and sampling frequency. In addition the regulation updates sampling frequencies for tritium and indicative dose (ID).

items.title: The Charities Act 2008 (Examination of Accounts) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: This Order amends section 65(5) of the Charities Act (Northern Ireland) 2008 to add two new bodies to the list of bodies whose members may examine the accounts of certain lower-income charities.

items.title: The Natural Mineral Water, Spring Water and Bottled Drinking Water Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: These Regulations implement and enforce the following European instruments—

items.title: The Private Water Supplies (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations amend the Private Water Supplies Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2009 (“the Principal Regulations”) with the aim of further protecting human health from the adverse effects of any contamination of water supplied for human consumption purposes. In particular, they make further provision to implement Council Directive 2013/51/Euratom laying down requirements for the protection of the health of the general public with regard to radioactive substances in water intended for human consumption. The Principal Regulations are amended in the following ways.

items.title: The Smoke Control Areas (Authorised Fuels) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: These Regulations amend the Smoke Control Areas (Authorised Fuels) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2013 (S.R. 2013 No. 205), to add two new fuels to the list of fuels declared to be authorised fuels, for the purposes of Article 2(2) of the Clean Air (Northern Ireland) Order 1981.

items.title: The Rules of the Court of Judicature (Northern Ireland) (Amendment No. 2) 2015
items.description: These Rules amend the Rules of the Court of Judicature (Northern Ireland) 1980 (S.R. 1980 No. 346) to amend Order 61A by substituting a new rule 3 to make provision for applications for leave to appeal to the High Court under sections 26, 28, 103, 105, 108 and 110 of the Extradition Act 2003 (c.41).

items.title: The Nitrates Action Programme (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations amend the Nitrates Action Programme Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2014 in regard to penalties and the measures concerning land management and record keeping.

items.title: The Loading Bays on Roads (Amendment) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
The Victim Charter (Justice Act (Northern Ireland) 2015) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: This Order brings the Victim Charter, which has been prepared and issued by the Department of Justice, into operation on 13th November 2015. The Charter is laid before the Assembly with this Order.

items.title: The Renewable Heat Incentive Schemes (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: These Regulations, which apply to Northern Ireland, amend the Renewable Heat Incentive Scheme Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2012 (the “2012 Regulations”) and the Domestic Renewable Heat Incentive Scheme (Northern Ireland) 2014 (the “2014 Regulations”). The 2012 Regulations established a renewable heat incentive scheme for non-domestic and the 2014 Regulations a renewable heat incentive scheme for domestic, under which owners of plants which generate heat from specified renewable sources and meet specified criteria may receive payments at prescribed tariffs for the heat used for eligible purposes. The 2012 Regulations and 2014 Regulations confer functions on the Department in connection with matters relating to the general administration of the schemes.

items.title: The Occupational Pension Schemes (Power to Amend Schemes to Reflect Abolition of Contracting-out) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: Section 24(2) of the Pensions Act (Northern Ireland) 2015 (“the Act”) gives employers sponsoring contracted-out salary related occupational pension schemes a power to amend the scheme rules to reflect the abolition of contracting-out for those schemes (“the power”). Amendments may be made to increase employee contributions or alter the future accrual of benefits, or do both, for or in respect of some or all of the scheme members. But the extent to which the scheme rules may be amended using this power is limited by reference to the increase in the sponsoring employer’s national insurance contributions due to the abolition of contracting-out.

items.title: The U125 Old Grand Jury Road, Saintfield (Abandonment) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
The U6076 Cloghanramer Road, Newry (Abandonment) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
The Roads (Speed Limit) (No. 4) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
The Human Trafficking and Exploitation (2015 Act) (Commencement No.1) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: Section 11 of, and Schedule 3 to, the Human Trafficking and Exploitation (Criminal Justice and Support for Victims) Act (Northern Ireland) 2015 make provision for courts in Northern Ireland to impose Slavery and Trafficking Prevention Orders (STPOs) upon offenders convicted of an offence of slavery, servitude and forced or compulsory labour, or human trafficking, either upon sentencing or following an application by the Police Service of Northern Ireland.

items.title: The Cycle Routes (Amendment No. 3) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
The Parking Places and Waiting Restrictions (Belfast City Centre) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
The Prohibition of Waiting (Schools) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
The Taxis (Antrim) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
The Parking Places on Roads (Disabled Persons’ Vehicles) (Amendment No. 3) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
The Parking and Waiting Restrictions (Belfast) (Amendment No. 4) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
The Bus Lanes (Upper Newtownards Road, Belfast – between Sandown Road and Knock Road) (Amendment) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
The Charities (2008 Act) (Commencement No. 7) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: This Order brings into operation provisions of the Charities Act (Northern Ireland) 2008 relating to charity accounts and reports. It also brings into operation an unrelated repeal.

items.title: The Charities (Accounts and Reports) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: These Regulations make provision in respect of the preparation and scrutiny of accounts prepared by charities and the preparation of annual reports by charities. The Regulations also make provision for the preparation and scrutiny of group accounts and the preparation of group annual reports by parent charities. The Regulations apply to charities registered in the register of charities kept under section 16 of the Charities Act (Northern Ireland) 2008 (“the Act”) and for financial years beginning on or after 1st January 2016.

items.title: The Charities Act 2008 (Substitution of Sums) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: This Order amends provisions of the Charities Act (Northern Ireland) 2008 (“the Act”) relating to the preparation and examination of charities’ accounts.

items.title: The Waste Management Licensing (Amendment No.2) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: These Regulations amend regulation 2 of and Schedule 1 to the Controlled Waste (Registration of Carriers and Seizure of Vehicles) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1999 (the 1999 Regulations). They also amend regulation 2 of the Waste Management Licensing Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2003 (the 2003 Regulations) which prescribes offences which must be taken into account by the Department when determining if a person can be deemed a fit and proper person to hold a waste management licence. The Regulations also introduce a new Schedule 6 to the 2003 Regulations, to provide an updated list of offences that includes environmental, financial and other relevant offences.

items.title: The Storage of Carbon Dioxide (Licensing etc.) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: These Regulations form part of the implementation in Northern Ireland of Directive 2009/31/EC of the European Parliament and of the Council of 23 April 2009 on the geological storage of carbon dioxide (OJ No L140, 5.6.2009, p. 114) (“the Directive”).

items.title: The Storage of Carbon Dioxide (Access to Infrastructure) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: These Regulations form part of the implementation in Northern Ireland of Directive 2009/31/EC of the European Parliament and of the Council of 23 April 2009 on the geological storage of carbon dioxide (OJ No L 140, 5.6.2009, p. 114) (“the Directive”). They implement Articles 21 and 22 of the Directive on third party access to carbon dioxide storage sites and transport networks.

items.title: The Taxis (2008 Act) (Commencement No. 5) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: This Order brings into operation the provisions of the Taxis Act (Northern Ireland) 2008 specified in—

items.title: The Planning General Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: Regulations 2 to 10 apply Parts 3, 4 (except chapters 1 and 2 of that Part) and 5 (except sections 157 to 163) of the Planning Act (Northern Ireland) 2011 with modifications to—

items.title: The Clifton Street/Carrick Hill, Belfast (Footway) (Abandonment) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
The Loading Bays on Roads (Amendment No. 3) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
The Carnbane Road, Newry (Abandonment) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
The Taxi Licensing Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: These Regulations are made under the Taxis Act (Northern Ireland) 2008 (2008 c.4 (N.I)) which provides for the regulation and testing of vehicles which are to be licensed as taxis.

items.title: The Taxis (Taximeters, Devices and Maximum Fares) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: These Regulations make provision under the Taxis Act (Northern Ireland) 2008 (2008 c.4 (N.I.)) for the inspection, testing and sealing of approved taximeters and devices and prescribe the fee payable. The Regulations replace the existing taximeter requirement in the Public Service (Conditions of Fitness, Equipment and Use) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1995.

items.title: The Taxi Operators Licensing (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: These Regulations make minor amendments to the Taxi Operators Licensing Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2012, as a consequence of the Taxi Licensing Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015.

items.title: The Taxi Accessibility Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: These Regulations apply to certain taxis which are Class B licensed taxis.

items.title: The Road Vehicles Lighting (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: These Regulations amend the Road Vehicles Lighting Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2000 by including a yellow light from a taxi roof sign in the list of exceptions at regulation 13(2).

items.title: The Planning (Fees) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: These Regulations correct a drafting error in the Planning (Fees) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015 and clarify how fees for mixed development applications should be calculated.

items.title: The European Maritime and Fisheries Fund (Financial Assistance) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: These Regulations provide that the Department may pay financial assistance for the purposes of Title V of Regulation (EU) No 508/2014 of the European Parliament and of the Council on the European Maritime and Fisheries Fund (OJ.No. L149, 20.5.2014, p.1) (the “EMFF Regulation”).

items.title: The Motor Vehicles (Driving Instruction) (Trainee Licence) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: These Regulations amend the Motor Vehicles (Driving Instruction) (Trainee Licence) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2010 in order to require trainee instructors to wear the trainee licence or display the trainee licence in the motor car in such position and manner as to be plainly and distinctly visible when giving instruction.

items.title: The Planning (Use Classes) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: This Order replaces the Planning (Use Classes) Order (Northern Ireland) 2004.

items.title: The Parking and Waiting Restrictions (Strabane) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
The Waiting Restrictions (Portballintrae) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
The Payment of Pension Levies for Past Periods Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: These Regulations implement, in respect of payments of pension levies under the Pensions (Northern Ireland) Order 2005 (S.I. 2005/255 (N.I. 1) and regulations made under it, in respect of periods before the coming into operation of the Pension Protection Fund and Occupational Pension Schemes (Miscellaneous Amendments) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2010 (S.R. 2010 No. 32) and the Occupational Pension Schemes (Levies) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2010 (S.R. 2010 No. 249), the Decision of the European Commission 55/2007/EC of 11th February 2009 (O.J. No. L242, 15.9.09, p.21) that the exemption from the payment of a levy to the Pension Protection Fund corresponding to the pension liabilities covered by a Crown guarantee constitutes State aid that is incompatible with the common market within the meaning of Article 107 of the Treaty on the Functioning of the European Union.

items.title: The Occupational Pensions (Revaluation) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: This Order, which corresponds to an Order (S.I. 2015/1916) made by the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions under paragraph 2(1) of Schedule 3 to the Pension Schemes Act 1993, specifies appropriate revaluation percentages. The percentages specified are relevant to the revaluation of benefits under occupational pension schemes, as required by section 80 of, and Schedule 2 to, the Pension Schemes (Northern Ireland) Act 1993. The lower revaluation percentage introduced by the Pensions (No. 2) Act (Northern Ireland) 2008 (c. 13 (N.I.)) does not apply to pensionable service before 6th April 2009.

items.title: The Social Security (Crediting and Treatment of Contributions, and National Insurance Numbers) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: These Regulations amend the Social Security (Crediting and Treatment of Contributions, and National Insurance Numbers) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2001 (“the Crediting and Treatment Regulations”) to disapply the requirement to apply for a National Insurance number in the case of a person in respect of whom the Department for Social Development or the Commissioners of Her Majesty’s Revenue and Customs are notified that a biometric immigration document is to be issued under regulation 13A of the Immigration (Biometric Registration) Regulations 2008 (S.I. 2008/3048 as amended by S.I. 2015/433) (“the 2008 Regulations”). Regulation 13A covers cases involving an application for entry clearance. There is already a similar disapplication for cases where a biometric immigration document is issued under regulation 13 of the 2008 Regulations.

items.title: The Firearms (Variation of Fees) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: This Order varies certain fees payable under the Firearms (Northern Ireland) Order 2004.

items.title: The Smoke Control Areas (Exempted Fireplaces) (Amendment No.2) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: The Smoke Control Areas (Exempted Fireplaces) (No.2) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2013 (“the 2013 Regulations”) specify those fireplaces exempted from the provisions of Article 17(7) of the Clean Air (Northern Ireland) Order 1981 (“the 1981 Order”).

items.title: The Disabled Persons (Badges for Motor Vehicles) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Disabled Persons (Badges for Motor Vehicles) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1993.

items.title: The Common Agricultural Policy (Review of SCMO Decisions) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: These Regulations enable the Department to establish procedures for the review of a relevant determination made by the Department under any of the EU instruments listed in the Schedule to the Regulations, or any statutory provision which implements or supplements those EU instruments in Northern Ireland.

items.title: The Control of Traffic (Alfred Street/Upper Arthur Street, Belfast) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
The Motor Vehicle Testing (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: These Regulations amend the Motor Vehicle Testing Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2003 to remove the requirement to display a vehicle test disc.

items.title: The Parking Places and Waiting Restrictions (Alfred Street/Upper Arthur Street, Belfast) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
The Pensions (2015 Act) (Consequential, Supplementary and Incidental Amendments) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: This Order makes consequential, supplementary and incidental amendments in relation to the provisions of Part 1 of the Pensions Act (Northern Ireland) 2015 (“the 2015 Act”), which creates a new state pension for people reaching pensionable age on or after 6th April 2016.

items.title: The HIV Testing Kits and Services (Revocation) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: These Regulations revoke the HIV Testing Kits and Services Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1992 (the “1992 Regulations”) in relation to Northern Ireland only. The 1992 Regulations make it an offence, subject to various exceptions:

items.title: The Control of Traffic (Albert Street/Durham Street/College Square North, Belfast) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
The Parking Places on Roads (Coaches) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
The Rules of the Court of Judicature (Northern Ireland) (Amendment No. 3) 2015
items.description: These Rules amend the Rules of the Court of Judicature (Northern Ireland) 1980 (S.R. 1980 No.346) by amending Order 29 to make provision for the High Court to make an order for an interim payment to be made to a plaintiff in cases in which there is more than one defendant.

items.title: The Roads (Speed Limit) (No. 5) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
The Concession Road, Route A37, Clonalig (Part-Time 20mph Speed Limit) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
The Justice (2015 Act) (Commencement No. 4) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: This Order brings into operation certain provisions of the Justice Act (Northern Ireland) 2015 (c.9 (N.I.)) (“the Act”).

items.title: The Police Service of Northern Ireland (Amendment) Regulations 2015
items.description: These Regulations amend the Police Service of Northern Ireland Regulations 2005 in respect of the periods of maternity and adoption leave to be taken into account in reckoning the length of service of a member of the Police Service of Northern Ireland, for the purpose of determining the member’s pay.

items.title: The Public Service Vehicles (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: These Regulations amend the Public Service Vehicles Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1985 to remove the requirement to display a vehicle test disc on a bus.

items.title: The Firefighters’ Pension Scheme (Transitional and Consequential Provisions) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: These Regulations make transitional provisions for a member of the Firefighters’ Pension Scheme (FPS) and a member of the New Firefighters’ Pension Scheme (NFPS) who are no longer able to remain active members of those Schemes following the transitional provisions in Schedule 2 to the Firefighters’ Pension Scheme Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015 (2015 Regulations).

items.title: The Firefighters’ Pension Scheme (Amendment) (No. 3) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: This Order amends the Firefighters’ Pension Scheme Order (Northern Ireland) 2007. Part A1 of Schedule 7 to the Scheme sets out the contribution rates payable by members which are determined by reference to the applicable pensionable pay band. This Order substitutes the table in Part A1 to provide an annual one per cent uplift in the pay bands until 2018.

items.title: The Firefighters’ Compensation Scheme (Amendment) (No. 2) and the New Firefighters’ Pension Scheme (Amendment) (No. 4) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: This Order amends the Annex to the Firefighters’ Compensation Scheme Order (Northern Ireland) 2007 which provides for a compensation scheme for firefighters and dependants of firefighters in Northern Ireland (“the Compensation Scheme”). The amendments to the Compensation Scheme set out in Schedule 1 to this Order are consequential on the coming into operation of the Firefighters’ Pension Scheme 2015 (“the 2015 Scheme”) set out in the Firefighters’ Pension Scheme Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015.

items.title: The Parking Places on Roads and Waiting Restrictions (Newry) (Amendment) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
The Parking Places on Roads (Disabled Persons’ Vehicles) (Amendment No. 4) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
The Fluorinated Greenhouse Gases Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: The Regulations revoke and remake with amendments the Fluorinated Greenhouse Gases Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2009 (S.R. 2009 No. 184).

items.title: The Goods Vehicles (Testing) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: These Regulations amend the Goods Vehicles (Testing) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2003 to remove the requirement to display a vehicle test disc.

items.title: The Local Government (Executive Arrangements) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: Part 6 of the Local Government Act (Northern Ireland) 2014 (“the 2014 Act”) (executive arrangements) provides for the discharge of a council’s functions by an executive of the council (which must take one of the forms specified in section 21(2) of that Act (council executives)) unless those functions are specified as functions that are not to be the responsibility of the council’s executive. These regulations specify the functions that are not to be the responsibility of a council’s executive or are to be the responsibility of an executive only to a limited extent or only in specified circumstances.

items.title: The Water Framework Directive (Priority Substances and Classification) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations replace Schedules 1, 2 and 3 of The Water Framework Directive (Priority Substances and Classification) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2011 and add a new Schedule to those Regulations. These Regulations set out the classification schemes used under the implementation of Directive 2000/60/EC establishing a framework for Community action in the field of water policy (O.J. No. L327, 22.12.2000 p.60).

items.title: The Rates (Owners Allowances) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: This Order-

items.title: The Rates (Exemption for Automatic Telling Machines in Rural Areas) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015 (revoked)
items.description: Article 42(1F) and (1G) of the Rates (Northern Ireland) Order 1977 (“the 1977 Order”) provides that there shall be distinguished in the NAV list as wholly exempt from rates any automatic telling machine which is situated in a rural area during a financial year ending before such date as is specified by an Order made by the Department of Finance and Personnel. 1st April 2016 is specified by this Order.

items.title: The Rates (Temporary Rebate) (Amendment) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: Article 31D of the Rates (Northern Ireland) Order 1977 provides for a rebate on occupied rates for retail properties. This rebate applies to properties which become occupied during the three year period ending on 31st March 2015 after having been unoccupied for a continuous period of at least twelve months. The amount of the rebate is one half of the rates chargeable in respect of the net annual value of the property and is granted for a period of twelve months.

items.title: The Planning (2011 Act) (Commencement No.3) and (Transitional Provisions) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: This Order is the third commencement order made under the Planning Act (Northern Ireland) 2011 (c.25) (2011 Act).

items.title: The Motor Vehicles (Driving Instruction) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: These Regulations amend the Motor Vehicles (Driving Instruction) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2010 in order to require instructors of category B+E vehicles to be registered as Approved Driving Instructors within a specified time period. The Regulations introduce a facility for disabled drivers to take the driving instruction tests in a car with automatic transmission and stipulate that if an Approved Driving Instructor passes their test in a vehicle with a automatic transmission, they can only give instruction in a vehicle with automatic transmission. The Regulations also specify the documents that are to be regarded as guidance issued by the Registrar in accordance with Article 52(4) of the Road Traffic (Northern Ireland) Order 2007 and reduce the fee payable for submission to theory test from £72 to £66 from 28th February 2015 and to £64 from 1st October 2015.

items.title: The Attorney General’s Human Rights Guidance (Youth Justice Agency – Conditions of Detention) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: This Order brings into operation guidance by the Attorney General for Northern Ireland, pursuant to section 8 of the Justice (Northern Ireland) Act 2004, which was laid before the Assembly on 15th December 2014.

items.title: The Attorney General’s Human Rights Guidance (Probation Board for Northern Ireland) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: This Order brings into operation guidance by the Attorney General for Northern Ireland, pursuant to section 8 of the Justice (Northern Ireland) Act 2004, which was laid before the Assembly on 15th December 2014.

items.title: The Social Security (Industrial Injuries) (Prescribed Diseases) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: These Regulations amend the Social Security (Industrial Injuries) (Prescribed Diseases) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1986 which prescribe diseases for which industrial injuries benefit is payable.

items.title: The Misuse of Drugs (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: These Regulations amend the Misuse of Drugs Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2002. The Schedule of the Regulations in which a controlled drug is placed affects the extent to which the drug can be lawfully imported, exported, produced, supplied or possessed. The controlled drugs placed in Schedule 1 to the Regulations are those subject to the tightest controls.

items.title: The Misuse of Drugs (Designation) (Amendment) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: Section 7(3) of the Misuse of Drugs Act 1971 requires regulations to be made to allow the use for medical purposes of the drugs which are subject to control under that Act. Section 7(3) does not apply to any drug designated by order under section 7(4) as a drug to which section 7(4) is to apply. This Order amends the Misuse of Drugs (Designation) Order (Northern Ireland) 2001 by inserting the synthetic opiate (known as AH-7921) and LSD related compounds into Part 1 of the Schedule to that Order. This Order also replaces the definition of tryptamine compounds in Part 1 of the Schedule with a wider generic definition.

items.title: The Inquiry into Historical Institutional Abuse (Amendment of Terms of Reference) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: This Order amends the Terms of Reference of the Historical Institutional Abuse Inquiry 1922 to 1995.

items.title: The Health and Personal Social Services (Superannuation), Health and Social Care (Pension Scheme) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Health and Personal Social Services (Superannuation) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1995 (S.R. 1995 No.95) (“the 1995 Regulations”) and the Health and Social Care (Pension Scheme) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008 (S.R. 2008 No.256) (“the 2008 Regulations”).

items.title: The Child Maintenance (2008 Act) (Commencement No. 15) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: This Order provides for the coming into operation of section 31 of the Child Maintenance Act (Northern Ireland) 2008, on the day after the day on which this Order is made for the purpose of making of regulations and on 23rd March 2015 for all other purposes. Section 31 inserts new Article 45D into the Child Support (Northern Ireland) Order 1991. Article 45D allows the Department to disclose information relating to persons who are liable to pay Child Support Maintenance to credit reference agencies. It will only allow the Department to disclose information relating to such a person where that person has given their consent to the disclosure or is subject to a liability order. Article 45D enables the Department to make regulations prescribing the information that may be disclosed.

items.title: The Planning (Claims for Compensation) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: The purpose of these Regulations is to prescribe the time within and the manner in which claims for compensation for loss or damage caused by the service of a building preservation notice shall be made to a council.

items.title: The One-Way Traffic (Belfast) (Amendment) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
The Motor Vehicles (Driving Licences) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: These Regulations make further provision to implement Directive 2006/126/EC of the European Parliament and of the Council of 20th December 2006 on driving licences (“the Third Driving Licences Directive”) (O.J. L403, 30.12.2006, p. 18).

items.title: The Parking and Waiting Restrictions (Belfast) (Amendment) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
The Planning (Hazardous Substances) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015 (revoked)
items.description: Section 108 of the Planning Act (Northern Ireland) 2011 (the  “2011 Act”) provides that the presence on, over or under land, of a hazardous substance equal to or in excess of the controlled quantity (as specified by regulations) requires the consent of the council.

items.title: The Planning (Local Development Plan) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: Part 2 of the Planning Act (Northern Ireland) 2011 (the 2011 Act) establishes a new system of local development planning in Northern Ireland. These Regulations make provision for the operation of that system.

items.title: The Planning (Statement of Community Involvement) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: Section 4 of the Planning Act (Northern Ireland) 2011 places a statutory duty a council to prepare a statement of community involvement (SCI).

items.title: The Pneumoconiosis, etc., (Workers’ Compensation) (Payment of Claims) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: Under the Pneumoconiosis, etc., (Workers’ Compensation) (Northern Ireland) Order 1979 (“the Order”) lump sum payments may be made to certain persons disabled by a disease to which the Order applies, or to dependants of persons who were so disabled before they died.

items.title: The Mesothelioma Lump Sum Payments (Conditions and Amounts) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: These Regulations amend the Mesothelioma Lump Sum Payments (Conditions and Amounts) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008 to increase amounts of lump sums payable to people with mesothelioma or to their dependants under the Mesothelioma, etc., Act (Northern Ireland) 2008. The increase in each case is 1.2 per cent., rounded up or down to the nearest £1 as appropriate.

items.title: The Planning (Control of Advertisements) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: These Regulations replace the Planning (Control of Advertisements) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1992 (“the 1992 Regulations”).

items.title: The Parking and Waiting Restrictions (Londonderry) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
The Local Government Reorganisation (Compensation for Loss of Employment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: These regulations provide for compensation for certain persons affected by the local government reorganisation.

items.title: The Teachers’ Superannuation (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on 1st April 2015, amend the Teachers’ Superannuation Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1998.

items.title: The Firefighters’ Compensation Scheme (Amendment) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: This Order further amends the Annex to the Firefighters’ Compensation Scheme Order (Northern Ireland) 2007 in which is set out the compensation scheme for firefighters and dependants of firefighters in Northern Ireland.

items.title: The Planning (General Permitted Development) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: The main purpose of this Order is to grant planning permission for certain classes of development under Part 3 of the Planning Act (Northern Ireland) 2011, subject to Articles 3 to 7.

items.title: The Planning (Development Management) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: These Regulations make provision for the new development management processes for determining planning applications. The statutory rule puts in place the regulatory framework required to implement the development management provisions in Part 3 of the Planning Act (Northern Ireland) 2011 (“the 2011 Act”).

items.title: The Planning (General Development Procedure) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: This Order revokes the Planning (General Development) Order (Northern Ireland) 1993 (“the 1993 Order”) and subsequent amendment orders.

items.title: The Planning (Fees) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: These Regulations prescribe the fee payable in respect of applications made under the Planning Act (Northern Ireland) 2011.

items.title: The Planning (Environmental Impact Assessment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: These Regulations revoke and replace the Planning (Environmental Impact Assessment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2012 (“the 2012 Regulations”). The 2012 Regulations consolidated and amended the Planning (Environmental Impact Assessment) Regulations 1999 which implemented Council Directive 85/337/EEC (O.J. No. L. 175, 5.7.1985, p 40 (“the EIA Directive”)) on the assessment of the effects of certain public and private projects on the environment. The EIA Directive was amended by Directive 97/11/EC, (O.J. No. L 73, 14.3.1997, p.5); Directive 2003/35/EC, (O.J. No. L 156, 25.6.2003, p.17); and Directive 2009/31/EC, (O.J. No. 140, 5.6.2009, p.114). It was then repealed and replaced by Directive 2011/92/EU (O.J. No. L26, 28.1.2012, p.1) which entered into force on 17th February 2012.

items.title: The Rates (Regional Rates) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: This Order fixes the amount of the regional rate for the year ending 31st March 2016. It fixes 31.86 pence in the pound as the amount of the regional rate to be levied on the rateable net annual values of hereditaments (“non-domestic regional rate”) and 0.4042 pence in the pound as the amount of the regional rate to be levied on the rateable capital values of hereditaments (“domestic regional rate”). This applies an increase of 1.4 per cent. after the effects of the non-domestic revaluation exercise are taken into account. The non-domestic revaluation will come into operation on 1st April 2015.

items.title: The Judicial Pensions Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: Part 2 of these Regulations establishes a scheme for the payment of pensions and other benefits to members of the judiciary. The scheme so established is a career average revalued earnings scheme.

items.title: The Local Government Pension Scheme (Governance) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: These Regulations amend the Local Government Pension Scheme Regulations 2014 (“the Principal Regulations”) to make provision for the governance arrangements of the Local Government Pension Scheme (“the Scheme”).

items.title: The Firefighters' Pension Scheme Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: Part 1 of these Regulations establishes a scheme for the payment of pensions and other benefits to firefighters in Northern Ireland from 1st April 2015. The scheme so established is a career average revalued earnings scheme.

items.title: The Pension Protection Fund and Occupational Pension Schemes (Levy Ceiling) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015 (revoked)
items.description: This Order, which corresponds to provisions of an Order (S.I. 2015/66) made by the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions under section 178 of the Pensions Act 2004, specifies, for the purposes of Article 160 of the Pensions (Northern Ireland) Order 2005 (“the 2005 Order”), the levy ceiling for the financial year beginning on 1st April 2015.

items.title: The Firefighters’ Pension Scheme (Amendment) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: This Order amends the Firefighters’ Pension Scheme Order (Northern Ireland) 2007.

items.title: The Employer's Liability (Compulsory Insurance) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations amend the Employer's Liability (Compulsory Insurance) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1999 (“the 1999 Regulations”). The 1999 Regulations specify employers who are exempted from the requirements of Part III of the Employer's Liability (Defective Equipment and Compulsory Insurance) (Northern Ireland) Order 1972 (“the 1972 Order”) to insure and maintain insurance against liability for personal injury suffered by their employees and arising out of and in the course of their employment. The specified employers are certain public bodies; the specified classes of employer include any employers to the extent that they are required to insure under a compulsory motor insurance scheme by virtue of the fact that their employees are carried on, or are alighting from or are entering into, a motor vehicle. The exemptions specified in the 1999 Regulations are additional to those contained in Article 7 of the 1972 Order.

items.title: The Public Service (Civil Servants and Others) Pensions (Consequential Provisions) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: These Regulations make consequential provision in relation to public service pensions for civil servants and other public service workers and amend a previous set of regulations.

items.title: The Rates (Social Sector Value) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015 (revoked)
items.description: The Rates (Social Sector Value) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2007 (“the 2007 Regulations”) provide, subject to exceptions, for a person to be chargeable to rates in respect of a domestic property owned by the Northern Ireland Housing Executive or a housing association listed in Schedule 1 to the Regulations, on the basis of social sector value rather than on the basis of the property’s rateable capital value. In determining a social sector value, the Department of Finance and Personnel is under a duty, by virtue of Article 23A(2) of the Rates (Northern Ireland) Order 1977, to ensure that the amount of rates chargeable is such proportion of any rent payable in respect of the property as the Department considers appropriate.

items.title: The Rates (Transitional Relief) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: Changes to local government districts, involving a reduction in the number of district councils from 26 to 11, take effect on 1st April 2015. This Order provides for transitional rate relief to ensure that there is no sudden and excessive increase in the district rates payable in respect of any property arising out of local government reorganisation. The relief takes the form of reductions in the amounts chargeable in respect of district rates. The reductions will be progressively decreased over a four year period and end on 31st March 2019.

items.title: The Planning (Trees) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: These Regulations replace the Planning (Trees) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2003.

items.title: The Planning (Management of Waste from Extractive Industries) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: These Regulations implement Directive 2006/21/EC (O.J. No.L102, 11.4.2006, P.15-34) (“the Mining Waste Directive”) of the European Parliament and of the Council of 15th March 2006 on the management of waste from extractive industries and amending Directive 2004/35/EC (O.J. No.L143, 30.4.2004, p.56-75) (“the Waste Framework Directive”). The Regulations replace the Planning (Management of Waste from Extractive Industries) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2010 (“the 2010 Regulations”) to take account of the role of district councils as local planning authorities and also for revisions to the EC Directives since the 2010 Regulations came into operation.

items.title: The Work and Families Act (Northern Ireland) 2015 (Commencement, Transitional Provisions and Savings) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: This Order commences the majority of provisions of the Work and Families Act (Northern Ireland) 2015.

items.title: The Paternity and Adoption Leave (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: These Regulations amend the Paternity and Adoption Leave Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2002 (“the 2002 Regulations”).

items.title: The Social Security Contributions and Benefits (Northern Ireland) Act 1992 (Application of Parts 12ZA and 12ZB to Adoptions from Overseas) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: These Regulations amend the Social Security Contributions and Benefits (Northern Ireland) Act 1992 (Application of Parts 12ZA and 12ZB to Adoptions from Overseas) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2003. The amendments made by these Regulations apply Part 12ZC (Statutory Shared Parental Pay) of the Social Security Contributions and Benefits (Northern Ireland) Act 1992, as modified by these Regulations, to adoptions from overseas, which are adoptions of children who enter Northern Ireland from outside the United Kingdom in connection with, or for the purposes of, adoption which does not involve the placement of a child for adoption under the law of any part of the United Kingdom.

items.title: The Statutory Paternity Pay and Statutory Adoption Pay (General) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: These Regulations amend the Statutory Paternity Pay and Statutory Adoption Pay (General) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2002 (S.R. No. 378) (“the Pay Regulations”).

items.title: The New Firefighters’ Pension Scheme (Amendment) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: This Order amends the Annex to the New Firefighters’ Pension Scheme Order (Northern Ireland) 2007. Some of the amendments make minor corrections to the New Firefighters’ Pension Scheme set out in that Annex. Most of the provisions amend the Scheme to provide those persons who were employed in Northern Ireland as retained firefighters during the period from 1st July 2000 to 5th April 2006 inclusive with access to a pension scheme for that period.

items.title: The Social Security Contributions and Benefits (Northern Ireland) Act 1992 (Application of Parts 12ZA, 12ZB and 12ZC to Parental Order Cases) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015
items.description: These Regulations apply Parts 12ZA, 12ZB and 12ZC of the Social Security Contributions and Benefits (Northern Ireland) Act 1992, as modified by these Regulations, to parental order parents. A parental order parent is a person who has applied, or intends to apply, with another person, under section 54 of the Human Fertilisation and Embryology Act 2008 for a parental order in respect of a child or someone who has such an order. Part 12ZA provides for entitlement to statutory paternity pay. Part 12ZB provides for entitlement to statutory adoption pay. Part 12ZC provides for entitlement statutory shared parental pay.

items.title: The Statutory Shared Parental Pay (Administration) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015
